sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution:
1. Authorizing the City Manager or designee to disburse up to $320,000 of Insight
Housing’s loan for Martin Luther King, Jr. (MLK) House for predevelopment;
2. Reserving $500,000 for the Northern Community Land Trust’s (NCLT)
rehabilitation of 2207 Haste Street; and
3. Authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute all original or amended
documents or agreements to effectuate these actions.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The attached resolution would allow disbursement of up to $320,000 of Insight’s existing
Housing Trust Fund (HTF) loan for the MLK House project for predevelopment. No new
funds are being added to the project. The resolution would also reserve $500,000 for
the rehabilitation of NCLT’s Haste Street property. Currently, there are sufficient funds
in the Housing Trust Fund (HTF) program to fund this request. Staff will determine the
exact mix of funds from the HTF closer to the loan closing, which may include Funds
#120, #122, #123, and #302.
sections.current_situation:
On December 2, 2024 the Housing Advisory Commission voted as follows to approve
the staff recommendations as written above.
M/S/C: Haycox/ Bell. Ayes: Bell, Braslaw, Haycox, Potter, Shere, and Twu. Abstain:
None. Absent: Mendonca (excused), Ortiz-Cedeño (excused), Simon-Weisberg
(excused).
The City of Berkeley received requests related to two Housing Trust Fund projects: MLK
House and Haste Street.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 5
HTF Recommendations for the MLK House Predevelopment Funds CONSENT CALENDAR
and Haste Street Rehabilitation Funds January 21, 2025
Insight requested an advance of $320,000 for the MLK House project to cover the cost
of predevelopment activities. Council approved a $2M loan in January 2024 to support
Insight’s acquisition and renovation of the property. The City does not typically release
funds (aside from acquisition) until all other funding is secured and the renovation is
moving forward. In the lead-up to the property rehab, Insight will have significant
carrying costs and design costs, and requested access to a portion of their loan to cover
those early project costs. Insight is not requesting new funding for MLK House.
NCLT submitted a request for $500,000 for the rehabilitation of the Haste Street
property (located at 2207 Haste Street). NCLT originally requested operating funding
through the City’s 2021 HTF Request for Proposals (RFP) process, but there were
insufficient funds available through the RFP to cover their request. Because the
property also had known capital needs, Council invited NCLT to submit a revised
request for up to $500,000 to support the renovation of the property.
sections.background:
MLK House
Located at 2942-2944 Martin Luther King Jr. Way, MLK House is a Single-Room
Occupancy (SRO) permanent housing project that will serve up to 11 formerly homeless
veterans. The property has 12 bedrooms (one of which will be used as an office), four
bathrooms, and a shared kitchen.
Insight recently took ownership from Resources for Community Development (RCD),
and assumed RCD’s original HTF loan. The transfer was authorized in January 2024, at
which point Council approved an additional $2M to Insight to renovate the property for
operation as housing for formerly homeless veterans. The proposed rehab scope
includes improvements to bathrooms, kitchens, HVAC, a new roof and windows,
landscaping, and accessibility upgrades. Insight will also add kitchenettes to each room
in order to make them eligible for Veterans Affairs Supportive Housing (VASH) vouchers
from the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Insight requested that $320K of the previously approved, $2M loan be provided as
predevelopment funding to pay for architectural, engineering, and permit fees needed in
advance of the rehabilitation work. There are limited non-City sources of funding for
predevelopment, and Insight is a smaller organization with limited cash resources to
front costs prior to start of construction. Council’s January 2024 funding approval did not
include an authorization to release funds for predevelopment activities.
Haste Street
Located at 2207 Haste Street, the property consists of eight bedrooms in a 3-story
building with a communal kitchen and three bathrooms. NCLT acquired the property in
1991 and was awarded a $154K HTF loan in 2000 to support property renovation. In
May 2022, NCLT entered into a lease with the Alameda County Homeless Action
Center (HAC). HAC operates the project and provides transitional supportive housing to

formerly
sections.rationale:
Council previously approved funding for Insight’s renovation of MLK House, and
approved NCLT returning with a request for up to $500,000 for the renovation of the
Haste Street property. These recommendations are in line with Council’s original
actions, and will allow the projects to move forward with renovations for projects that will
serve vulnerable populations (veterans and formerly homeless individuals).
sections.alternatives:
If Council does not approve the funding requests, Insight Housing and NCLT would
need to pursue alternative funding sources, which would likely increase project costs
and delay the renovations.
CONTACT PERSON
Asavari Devadiga, Community Development Project Coordinator, HHCS, 510-981-5426
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
APPROVAL OF MARTIN LUTHER KING JR. HOUSE PREDEVELOPMENT FUNDS
AND HASTE STREET REHABILITATION FUNDS
WHEREAS, City Council established a Housing Trust Fund (HTF) program to assist in
the development and expansion of housing affordable to low- and moderate-income
persons who either work or reside within the City of Berkeley, and authorized the City
Manager to implement the HTF program; and
WHEREAS, there is a great need for affordable and special needs housing in the City of
Berkeley as stated in the General Plan Housing Element and the City of Berkeley’s
Consolidated Plan; and
WHEREAS, City Council adopted guidelines (HTF Guidelines) as revised on January 19,
2021, and authorized the City Manager to implement the HTF program in accordance
with the Guidelines; and
WHEREAS, the Northern California Land Trust (NCLT) submitted an application for
operating subsidy for 2207 Haste Street through the 2021 HTF Request for Proposals
process, but were not funded due to insufficient funds; and
WHEREAS, on December 14, 2021, by Resolution 70,135-N.S., the City Council invited
NCLT to submit a revised funding application for up to $500,000 to support the renovation
of 2207 Haste Street; and
WHEREAS, on January 30, 2024, by Resolution 71,190-N.S., the City Council approved
the transfer of MLK House to Insight Housing and reserved $2,000,988 in HTF funds to
support the acquisition and renovation of the property; and
WHEREAS, in October 2024, NCLT submitted a request for $500,000 for the Haste Street
project and Insight submitted a request to use $320,000 of their existing loan for
predevelopment of the MLK House project; and
WHEREAS, on December 2, 2024, the Housing Advisory Commission recommended
Council approval to reserve $500,000 for NCLT’s rehabilitation of 2207 Haste Street, and
to advance up to $320,000 of Insight Housing’s loan for MLK House for predevelopment.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the Cit
